topic: Analemma figure-8 loop ratio 2.66 — c6 verification of c5 patch landings
change_summary: Decider commit 5ab70a1 (2026-05-24T09:26Z) patched 4 holes from c5 review: detail_verdict_text self-contradiction (major), epicycloid->hypocycloid (minor), tolerance arithmetic (minor), tldr_evidence area-to-perimeter inclusion (minor). Also updated globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json:spirograph_analysis.resulting_curve to 'hypocycloid'.
our_argument_summary: WIN-019 claims the analemma figure-8 has 'loop ratio 2.66'. The review argues: (a) 'loop ratio' is undefined in the standard analemma literature; (b) the five standard geometric ratios (lobe height 2.25, width 3.01, area 6.69, perimeter 2.56, area-to-perimeter 2.61) all miss 2.66 by more than the implied ±0.2% tolerance; (c) 2.66 matches the Spirograph gear ratio 8/3 = 2.66667 to within 0.25% and the dome's source labels the data as 'Spirograph'; (d) an 8/3-ratio hypocycloid Spirograph produces an 8-cusped curve, not a 2-lobed figure-8 — the named mechanism cannot even produce the observed shape; (e) post-hoc structural failure: 2.66 is hardcoded in inject_ai_layer.py with data_source='Spirograph' and never compared to any sky observation. Verdict: Misleading.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No analemma comparison logic exists in monitor.py. The value 2.66 is a static string in inject_ai_layer.py's confirmed_wins array with data_source='Spirograph'. The dome's pipeline never consults a sky observation for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded. Display only. No fetch, no comparison.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None applicable — no comparison logic exists for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The c5 patch did not touch code-level claims; they remain accurate.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no analemma domain (no fetch/comparison/validation). The value 2.66 lives only as a static string in inject_ai_layer.py's confirmed_wins array with data_source='Spirograph'. Verified at github.com/john09289/predictions/blob/main/inject_ai_layer.py snapshot accessed 2026-04.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics derives the analemma figure-8 from obliquity (23.44°) and eccentricity (0.0167); the asymmetry is real and documented (Meeus 1998; Holbrow 2013). But standard physics does NOT define a quantity called 'loop ratio' and certainly not one with value 2.66. The dome is not relabeling a standard quantity — it is asserting a new ratio that does not match any standard one.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The asymmetry of the analemma has been characterized since the 18th century (Meeus 1998 ch. 28). The dome's value 2.66 is a Spirograph gear ratio (8/3 = 2.66667), hardcoded with data_source='Spirograph'; nothing in the dome's pipeline compares it to a sky observation. The 'prediction' is post-hoc by construction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or sun_altitude (5,733 km) to 2.66 exists. The value is a free gear-ratio parameter (8/3) inside the Spirograph mechanism, not derived from dome geometry.

hole_0_major_self_contradiction.patch_landed: Removed 'textbook physics / trivial-transcription test' invocation. Replaced with structural post-hoc argument grounded in inject_ai_layer.py hardcoding (data_source='Spirograph') and pre-registration failure under the dome author's own timestamping definition. The new verdict's second paragraph is now CONSISTENT with detail_evidence, which establishes that 2.66 is a gear ratio, not a sky measurement. The closing 'No legitimate-novelty carve-out applies' line correctly grounds the carve-out denial in the gear-ratio provenance (not in a contradictory novelty claim).

hole_1_minor_hypocycloid.patch_landed: detail_evidence: '8-cusped epicycloid' -> '8-cusped hypocycloid'. globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json:spirograph_analysis.resulting_curve also updated to '8-cusped hypocycloid (hypocycloid with R/r = 8/3)'. Both surfaces consistent.

hole_2_minor_tolerance_arithmetic.patch_landed: Main 'must agree to within ±0.2%' threshold preserved. Main comparison sentence changed from 'misses by an order of magnitude in fractional terms (3.8% vs. 0.4%)' to 'misses by ~3.8%, an order of magnitude over the ±0.2% tolerance'. These two now read consistently against the ±0.2% half-width.
hole_2_minor_tolerance_arithmetic.residual:
The bullet list parenthetical for area-to-perimeter STILL contains '0.4% tolerance implied below' (verbatim from pre-patch text): '
  • lobe area-to-perimeter ratio ≈ 2.61 (deviation from 2.66: -1.9%, but this is a derived rather than a primitive metric, and 1.9% still exceeds the 0.4% tolerance implied below)
    • '. The paragraph that follows the bullet list now states ±0.2% (not 0.4%), so the 'implied below' anchor no longer matches what is actually implied below. This is the same kind of internal inconsistency the c5 hole flagged — partially closed.

hole_3_minor_tldr_cherry_pick.patch_landed: tldr_evidence list updated from '(height ~2.25, width ~3.01, perimeter ~2.56)' to '(height ~2.25, width ~3.01, perimeter ~2.56, area-to-perimeter ~2.61)'. Cherry-pick concern resolved.

holes_found.description: TOLERANCE ARITHMETIC — residual inside the bullet list. The area-to-perimeter bullet still says '1.9% still exceeds the 0.4% tolerance implied below' (data/wins.json:WIN-019.detail_evidence, area-to-perimeter <li>). The decider's patch correctly changed the main comparison sentence to use ±0.2% (the half-width of the 2.655–2.665 interval), but left the bullet's parenthetical untouched. So the bullet now references a tolerance value (0.4%) that is NOT implied by anything 'below' it — the text below the bullet only mentions ±0.2%. A reader scanning the bullets vs the paragraph will see two different tolerance numbers in the same paragraph and infer a math error. This is exactly the inconsistency the c5 hole was meant to eliminate; the patch closed it on the main sentence but not on the bullet.
holes_found.recommendation: One-character-class edit: in the area-to-perimeter <li>, change '0.4%' to '±0.2%' OR drop the trailing clause entirely so the bullet reads '... but this is a derived rather than a primitive metric.' The latter is cleaner because the 0.2%/0.4% (half-width vs full-width) distinction never carries any weight in the argument — the relevant point in the bullet is simply that area-to-perimeter is a derived metric. Either edit closes the residual without any other ripple. Update the c5 sister issue ISS-2179 (currently closed='fixed') with a note that the closure was premature, or open a new ISS for this residual.

kernel_of_truth.description: The analemma asymmetry is real and quantifiable (data/wins.json:WIN-019.detail_evidence, paragraph 1, cites Meeus 1998 ch.28, Holbrow 2013 arXiv:1302.0765, Lynch 2012 Irish Math. Soc. Bulletin 69, USNO Almanac, NOAA solcalc). The dome author is not inventing the figure-8 — that part is correct. And the underlying observation (south lobe larger than north lobe) is a fair thing to want a quantitative summary of.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being right that the asymmetry exists doesn't validate '2.66' as a measurement of it. None of the five well-defined ratios (height 2.25, width 3.01, area 6.69, perimeter 2.56, area-to-perimeter 2.61) matches 2.66 to the dome's own two-decimal precision (±0.2% half-width). The closest fit by two orders of magnitude is the Spirograph gear ratio 8/3 = 2.66667 (0.25% deviation), which the dome's own source code labels with data_source='Spirograph' (raw-text/inject_ai_layer.py confirmed_wins; verified against github.com/john09289/predictions/blob/main/inject_ai_layer.py). Worse, an 8/3-gear hypocycloid produces an 8-cusped curve, not a 2-lobed figure-8 (monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json:spirograph_analysis.resulting_curve='8-cusped hypocycloid'). So the named mechanism cannot even produce the observed shape, regardless of what 'loop ratio' is taken to mean.

deeper_analysis: The c5 patch closed the major hole cleanly. The new detail_verdict_text no longer claims 2.66 is 'textbook physics' (which contradicted the evidence) and now grounds the post-hoc failure in structural provenance — inject_ai_layer.py hardcoding with data_source='Spirograph', no pre-registration, and a gear-ratio source rather than a sky measurement. This is internally consistent with detail_evidence's three independent grounds for rejection. The hypocycloid/epicycloid terminology fix landed in both detail_evidence and globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json:spirograph_analysis.resulting_curve. The TLDR cherry-pick fix landed. The tolerance arithmetic is the one place the patch was incomplete: the main sentence now reads '±0.2%' consistently but the area-to-perimeter bullet still cites '0.4%' as 'implied below' — and what's below is ±0.2%. This is the only remaining minor inconsistency. There is no fresh adversarial hole that the c5 review missed; the dome defender's three best attacks ('loop ratio is our definition' — burden flipped in evidence §1; 'maybe perimeter is the match' — answered in the anticipated-objection paragraph; 'the gear ratio could just be coincidence' — answered by the 15× accuracy gap and the data_source self-label) are all handled. Citations re-spot-checked: arXiv:1302.0765 (Holbrow 2013), DOI 10.1017/CBO9781139020138 (Chave & Jones), Meeus 2nd ed. 1998, Lynch 2012 Irish Math. Soc. Bulletin 69 — all consistent with prior cycles' verifications. Verdict 'Misleading' continues to fit. Confidence 0.9 reflects that the patches mostly landed cleanly and the residual is a single-character-class edit, not a substantive argument weakness.
advocate_mode_notes: Best dome-defender attack on the current text: 'The 0.4% tolerance in the bullet shows you don't even agree with yourself about what tolerance you're applying — you're moving the goalposts within the same paragraph.' This is a real (though minor) rhetorical handhold. Closing the bullet residual removes the handhold.
cross_win_consistency_notes: No cross-WIN inconsistencies introduced. WIN-019's argument remains independent of WIN-008/009 (Schumann) and WIN-053 (analemma-adjacent if any). The spirograph_analysis fingerprint in monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json is now consistent with the rendered prose on the hypocycloid term.
quantitative_verification_notes: Numerical claims re-spot-checked: 8/3 = 2.66667 (0.25% deviation from 2.66): correct (|2.66 - 2.66667|/2.66 = 0.00251 = 0.25%). Perimeter 2.56 vs 2.66 deviation: |-2.56+2.66|/2.66 = 0.0376 = 3.76% ≈ 3.8%: correct. Implied interval at two-decimal precision: 2.655–2.665, half-width 0.005, fractional half-width = 0.005/2.66 = 0.00188 ≈ 0.2%: correct. Full-width = 0.01/2.66 = 0.376% ≈ 0.4%: this is the source of the residual confusion — the bullet's '0.4%' is the FULL-width fractional tolerance, the paragraph's '±0.2%' is the HALF-width. Both are technically correct but the bullet's text says '0.4% tolerance implied below' and what's actually below is the ±0.2% (half-width) framing, so the cross-reference is broken.

citations_verified: Meeus 1998 Astronomical Algorithms 2nd ed. ch. 28 — standard reference for Equation of Time and analemma
Holbrow 2013 arXiv:1302.0765 'Build your own analemma' — real preprint
Lynch 2012 Irish Math. Soc. Bulletin 69 pp. 47-56 'The equation of time and the analemma' — real publication
USNO Astronomical Almanac equation-of-time tables — standard reference
Part 3.1b #posthoc-standard anchor — internal cross-reference (verified to exist in V6 numbering)

summary_for_decider: PATCH VERIFICATION SUMMARY (qid=397, decider commit 5ab70a1): 3 of 4 holes patched cleanly (major self-contradiction, minor hypocycloid term, minor tldr cherry-pick). 1 hole partially patched (minor tolerance arithmetic): the main sentence and 'must agree' threshold are now consistent at ±0.2%, but the area-to-perimeter bullet still references '0.4% tolerance implied below' — and what is actually below is ±0.2%. This is a single-character-class edit (change '0.4%' to '±0.2%' OR drop the trailing clause). ISS-2179 (closed='fixed') was closed prematurely on this hole — decider should either re-open ISS-2179 with a residual note or open a new ISS for this residual. No fresh adversarial holes found; verdict 'Misleading' continues to hold. Carry-over: no open-issue currently tracks this residual (ISS-2179 is closed), so per Step 7c the decider should create one when integrating this review.
